{{Quote|Attuned to the Power of the Cosmos, my people traveled the galaxy via Infinity Gates—portals that transported us instantly between great distances. We observed many worlds and brought civilization to their people.|[[A'nang]]|<ref name="DOTJ 8">[[Dawn of the Jedi: The Prisoner of Bogan 3|''Dawn of the Jedi: The Prisoner of Bogan'' 3]]</ref>}}
The '''Infinity Gate''' was an ancient network of structures developed by the saurian [[Kwa/Legends|Kwa]] species of [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] during the [[Pre-Republic era]]. They enabled instantaneous interstellar travel between far-flung locations and could also be used as a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]].

==Description==
[[File:Kwa_Holdings.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A'nang and an Infinity Gate]]
Through what the [[Kwa/Legends|Kwa]] called the "[[The Force/Legends|power of the cosmos]]," the Infinity Gates were able to transport them across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]<ref name="DOTJ 8" /> or project devastating [[Infinity Wave]]s. The Gates were accessed from a [[Star Chamber (Star Temple)|Star Chamber]], within massive pyramidal structures known as the [[Star Temple]]s.<ref name="ff">{{FactFile|139|DAT 1-4|Dathomir}}</ref>

The Star Temples and its associated buildings contained traps which protected the Chambers from intruders. Inside the Star Chamber was a "realm of infinity," a pocket dimension that housed the central control station which was used to control the "power of infinity."<ref name="ff" />

==History==
[[File:KwaStarTemple-SWREP24.jpg|thumb|right|150px|A group of Kwa outside a Star Temple]]
During the [[Pre-Republic era]], the Kwa constructed the Infinity Gates, their design influenced by a [[Celestial/Legends|Celestial]] principle. The Infinity Gates enabled the Kwa to colonize other worlds within their [[Kwa holdings|region of space]]. However, their expansion brought them into conflict with the [[Gree/Legends|Gree]] [[Gree Enclave/Legends|Enclave]], which had developed similar devices known as [[hypergate]]s.<ref name="TEA" />

As the Kwa waned in power across the galaxy, they sealed the Infinity Gates and the Star Temples and set guardians to protect the realm of infinity—[[whuffa worm]]s with thick hides and impressive speed.<ref name="ff" /> Following their extermination by the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] and the conquest of Dathomir,<ref name="TEA" /> the surviving Kwa devolved into the primitive and semi-sentient [[Kwi]].<ref name="Infinity's End" />

In [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]], the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]] captured a local Star Temple and slaughtered many Kwi. The Nightsisters had intended to access the secrets of the Infinity Gate and use it to destroy [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. However, their plot was [[Mission to Dathomir (Post–Naboo crisis)|thwarted]] by the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], who, in his role as Temple Guardian, redirected the deadly wave aimed at Coruscant back at the Infinity Gate. Only the scattered ruins of the Star Temples were left. The Kwi also disappeared into the deserts.<ref name="ff" />

In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], [[Sigit Ranth]] investigated the Star Temple on Dathomir for the [[Imperial Remnant]], but finding no remains of the Star Chamber, abandoned the project, while the Remnant instead concentrated on recruiting Nightsisters as part of [[Project Nightsaber]].<ref name="Nightsaber">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031120nightsaber|text=Nightsaber|int=Nightsaber}}</ref>
